channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: SSS Enables Pensioners to Complete Annual Confirmation Online via Facial Verification
item.description: The Social Security System (SSS) has launched an online facial verification portal to make the Annual Confirmation of Pensioners (ACOP) process more convenient. This new system allows registered pensi...

item.title: Military Strikes Alone Cannot Ensure Political Stability in Iran, Lessons from Iraq
item.description: The article discusses the fundamental distinction between military success and political outcomes, using Iraq as a cautionary example for potential action against Iran. While the US achieved all milit...

item.title: UNSC Condemns Iran’s Gulf Attacks, Calls for Immediate Dialogue
item.description: On 12th March 2026, the UN Security Council (UNSC) adopted a resolution led by Bahrain condemning Iran's recent missile and drone strikes on Gulf countries, which targeted US bases and assets in retal...

item.title: Former Deputy Police Commissioner Francinah Vuma Testifies Before Parliamentary Committee on Corruption
item.description: Former deputy national police commissioner, Francinah Vuma, has appeared before Parliament’s ad hoc committee to address serious allegations of corruption within the criminal justice system. The commi...

item.title: Batang Quiapo Episode 797: Tanggol Prepares for the Confrontation in Manila
item.description: In the latest episode of 'Batang Quiapo' (Episode 797), Tanggol, portrayed by Coco Martin, intensifies his mission to confront the antagonist threatening Manila. The storyline focuses on Tanggol's str...

item.title: Rising Heat Poses Growing Health Risks for South African Children
item.description: As South Africa experiences increasingly intense heatwaves, experts warn that children are among the most vulnerable to the effects of extreme temperatures. In December 2025, Busisiwe Khumalo's 12-yea...

item.title: Severe Tornadoes Cause Fatalities and Damage in Indiana and Illinois
item.description: Multiple severe storms swept across northwest Indiana and northern Illinois on Tuesday, producing tornadoes that caused significant destruction and claimed at least two lives. In Lake Village, Indiana...

item.title: WhatsApp Introduces Parent-Managed Accounts for Children Under 13
item.description: Meta, the company behind WhatsApp, has announced a new feature that permits children under the age of 13 to use the messaging platform, but only through parent-managed accounts. Parents will create an...

item.title: Iran’s New Supreme Leader and Potential Impact on Global Oil Prices
item.description: The appointment of Mojtaba Khamenei as Iran’s new Supreme Leader introduces new uncertainties for global economics, particularly energy markets. Unlike his father, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, Mojtaba has ...

item.title: Tenant and Caretaker in Heated Dispute After Gas Cylinder Explosion
item.description: A recent gas cylinder explosion at a Nairobi home sparked a serious argument between the tenant and the building caretaker. The tenant suffered damages in her kitchen, including broken tiles and soot-...

item.title: Simba ends two-game draw streak with 2-1 win over Singida Black Stars
item.description: Simba SC finally returned to winning form after two consecutive draws in the Mainland Tanzania Premier League, securing a 2-1 victory over Singida Black Stars at Airtel Stadium on March 11, 2026. Anic...

item.title: Stakeholders Urge Stronger FCC Regulations to Protect Tanzanian Consumers
item.description: Stakeholders in Tanzania are pushing for stronger regulations and clearer procedures for the Fair Competition Commission (FCC) to ensure ordinary consumers can really benefit from the legal reforms ma...

item.title: IEA Coordinates Record Oil Reserve Release Amid Middle East Supply Disruption
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) announced a record coordinated release of 400 million barrels of strategic oil reserves to stabilize global supply chains disrupted by the conflict in the Middle ...

item.title: Cardinal David calls for fair tax system to support Filipinos amid global crisis
item.description: Kalookan Bishop Pablo Virgilio Cardinal David urged the Philippine government to ensure fairness in its tax system, emphasizing that it should serve the common good, especially as the ongoing conflict...

item.title: Oba Olu Falae vows to protect Yoruba land from criminals
item.description: Oba Olu Falae, the traditional ruler of Ilu-Abo in Akure North Local Government Area, don talk say Yoruba people no go gree make bandits or any external wahala take their land. As security wahala dey ...

item.title: NRL highlights missed ruck infringements as set restarts rise
item.description: The NRL has signalled that clubs should brace for greater scrutiny on ruck infringements after analysis revealed several missed penalties in round one, despite a 35 per cent rise in set restarts. Aver...

item.title: Japan and Germany release oil reserves to stabilise global energy markets
item.description: Japan and Germany have decided to release part of their strategic oil reserves following a sharp rise in global oil prices triggered by conflict in West Asia. Japan's Prime Minister Takaichi Sanae ann...

item.title: Kenya Champions African Investment in Space Technology for Disaster Resilience
item.description: Kenya is seriously pushing for African nations to boost their investments in space technology and robust data systems. It's all about getting better at handling disasters like floods and droughts, the...

item.title: Global Oil Shortage Persists Despite Unprecedented IEA Stockpile Release
item.description: The International Energy Agency (IEA) has announced an unprecedented emergency release of oil from the stockpiles of its member countries, totaling 400 million barrels, in response to recent disruptio...

item.title: Karakoram Highway Reopened After Landslides Blocked It in Diamer and Kohistan Amid Rain and Snowfall
item.description: The Karakoram Highway (KKH), a vital link connecting Gilgit-Baltistan with the rest of Pakistan, faced blockages at multiple points between Diamer in GB and Kohistan in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa due to lands...
